scope.context_note: Volume II section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Integers ($\mathbb{Z}$)', section 'Construction of $\mathbb{Z}$'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Integer Prepair
nodes.statement_display:
An \emph{integer prepair} is an ordered pair $(a,b)\in\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}$,
read as the formal difference $a-b$.
nodes.statement_tex:
An \emph{integer prepair} is an ordered pair $(a,b)\in\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}$,
read as the formal difference $a-b$.

direct_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

direct_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Integer Prepair Set
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The integer prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}:=\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Relation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Prepair Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integer prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}:=\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: Iterator Data
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

ancestor_tree.title: Quotient Set
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

nodes.title: Integer Class Notation
nodes.statement_display:
The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$-equivalence class of the
prepair $(a,b)$.
nodes.statement_tex:
The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$-equivalence class of the
prepair $(a,b)$.

direct_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Integers
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The integers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{Z}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}/{\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Prepair Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integer prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}:=\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{Z}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}/{\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

ancestor_tree.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

ancestor_tree.title: Integers
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: The integers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{Z}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}/{\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}}. \]

dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Relation

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

direct_dependencies.title: Integer Class Notation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$-equivalence class of the prepair $(a,b)$.

direct_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Class Notation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$-equivalence class of the prepair $(a,b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Prepair Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integer prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}:=\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integers are the quotient \[ \mathbb{Z}:=\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}/{\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Variable

dependencies.title: Integers

dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

ancestor_tree.title: Integer Class Notation
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: The notation $[a,b]$ denotes the $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$-equivalence class of the prepair $(a,b)$.

dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

direct_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Define $+_{\mathbb{W}}:\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}\to\mathbb{W}$ by \[ 0+_{\mathbb{W}}a=a,\qquad a+_{\mathbb{W}}0=a, \] and, for $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, \[ a+_{\mathbb{W}}b=a+_{\mathbb{N}}b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Integer Prepair Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The integer prepair set is \[ \operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}:=\mathbb{W}\times\mathbb{W}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Whole Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\mathbb{N}$ be the one-based natural-number system developed in the preceding chapter. Let $0$ be a new element with $0\notin\mathbb{N}$. Define \[ \mathbb{W}\coloneqq \mathbb{N}\cup\{0\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

ancestor_tree.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: For $(a,b),(c,d)\in\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$, define \[ (a,b)\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}(c,d) \quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+d=c+b. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function

dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation

dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on $\mathbb{W}$ Satisfies Cancellation

nodes.title: Formal-Difference Equivalence Is an Equivalence Relation
nodes.statement_display:
The relation $\sim_{\mathbb{Z}}$ is an equivalence relation on
$\operatorname{Pre}\mathbb{Z}$.
